segments.text: "Now there was a man named Elkanah who was from Ramathaim-zophim[^1] in the hill country of Ephraim. He was the son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu,[^2] the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.

footnotes.text: + Or from Ramathaim, a Zuphite; see LXX and 1 Chronicles 6:26 and 35.
+ Elihu is also called Eliab and Eliel; see 1 Chronicles 6:27 and 34.

segments.text: "He had two wives, one named Hannah and the other Peninnah. And Peninnah had children, but Hannah had none.
"Year after year Elkanah would go up from his city to worship and sacrifice to the LORD of Hosts at Shiloh, where Eli’s two sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were priests to the LORD.
"And whenever the day came for Elkanah to present his sacrifice, he would give portions to his wife Peninnah and to all her sons and daughters.
"But to Hannah he would give a double portion,[^3] for he loved her even though the LORD had closed her womb.
"Because the LORD had closed Hannah’s womb, her rival would provoke her and taunt her viciously.
"And this went on year after year. Whenever Hannah went up to the house of the LORD, her rival taunted her until she wept and would not eat.
Hannah, why are you crying?
segments.speaker: Elkanah

segments.text: her husband Elkanah asked.
Why won’t you eat? Why is your heart so grieved? Am I not better to you than ten sons?
segments.speaker: Elkanah

segments.text: "So after they had finished eating and drinking in Shiloh, Hannah stood up. Now Eli the priest was sitting on a chair by the doorpost of the temple of the LORD.
"In her bitter distress, Hannah prayed to the LORD and wept with many tears.
"And she made a vow, pleading,

O LORD of Hosts, if only You will look upon the affliction of Your maidservant and remember me, not forgetting Your maidservant but giving her a son, then I will dedicate him to the LORD all the days of his life, and no razor shall ever come over his head.
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: "As Hannah kept on praying before the LORD, Eli watched her mouth.

"Hannah was praying in her heart, and though her lips were moving, her voice could not be heard.
So Eli thought she was drunk
"and said to her,
How long will you be drunk? Put away your wine!
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: No, my lord,
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: Hannah replied.
I am a woman oppressed in spirit. I have not had any wine or strong drink, but I have poured out my soul before the LORD.
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: Do not take your servant for a wicked woman; for all this time I have been praying out of the depth of my anguish and grief.
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: Go in peace,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Eli replied,
and may the God of Israel grant the petition you have asked of Him.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: May your maidservant find favor with you,
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: said Hannah. Then she went on her way, and she began eating again, and her face was no longer downcast.

"The next morning Elkanah and Hannah got up early to bow in worship before the LORD, and then returned home to Ramah.
And Elkanah had relations with his wife Hannah, and the LORD remembered her.
"So in the course of time, Hannah conceived and gave birth to a son. She named him Samuel,[^4] saying,
Because I have asked for him from the LORD.
segments.speaker: Hannah

footnotes.text: + Samuel sounds like the Hebrew for heard of God.

segments.text: "Then Elkanah and all his house went up to make the annual sacrifice to the LORD and to fulfill his vow,
"but Hannah did not go.
After the boy is weaned,
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: she said to her husband,
I will take him to appear before the LORD and to stay there permanently.
segments.speaker: Hannah

footnotes.text: + MT; DSS include I will offer him as a Nazirite for all time.

segments.text: Do what you think is best,
segments.speaker: Elkanah

segments.text: her husband Elkanah replied,
and stay here until you have weaned him. Only may the LORD confirm His word.
segments.speaker: Elkanah

segments.text:
[^6]
So Hannah stayed and nursed her son until she had weaned him.

footnotes.text: + MT; DSS, LXX, and Syriac your word

segments.text: "Once she had weaned him, Hannah took the boy with her, along with a three-year-old bull,[^7] an ephah of flour,[^8] and a skin of wine. Though the boy was still young, she brought him to the house of the LORD at Shiloh.

footnotes.text: + DSS, LXX, and Syriac; MT three bulls
+ An ephah is approximately 20 dry quarts or 22 liters (probably about 25.5 pounds or 11.6 kilograms of flour).

segments.text: "And when they had slaughtered the bull, they brought the boy to Eli.
Please, my lord,
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: said Hannah,
as surely as you live, my lord, I am the woman who stood here beside you praying to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: I prayed for this boy, and since the LORD has granted me what I asked of Him,
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: I now dedicate the boy to the LORD. For as long as he lives, he is dedicated to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Hannah

segments.text: So they worshiped[^9] the LORD there.

footnotes.text: + One DSS manuscript; MT he worshiped
